scope_note: QA Witt Tower Tohoku Aftershock Orbit Discriminator cert [448]. Empirical cert applying the Witt tower three-tier orbit partition (MOD=27, T0=bins 0-8 Singularity neighbourhood, T1=bins 9-17 Satellite neighbourhood, T2=bins 18-26 Cosmos neighbourhood) to earthquake catalog data from the 2011-03-11 Tohoku M9.1 aftershock sequence. Data: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program ComCat (doi:10.5066/F7MS3QZH, public domain); M>=3.0 events within bounding box 35-42N 138-146E. Signal feature: M>=3.0 earthquake count per 6-hour window — a natural integer (Poisson count). Background epoch: 2011-02-01 to 2011-03-08 (35 days, 140 windows, 21 total events). Aftershock epoch: 2011-03-11 to 2011-03-18 (7 days, 28 windows, 1965 total events). All windows rank-normalised to Z/27Z across the combined pool of 168 windows. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) 140 background + 28 aftershock windows PASS. (C2) ALL 28 aftershock windows excluded from T0; hypergeometric log10_p=-5.50 PASS. (C3) ALL 28/28 aftershock windows in T2; hypergeometric log10_p=-15.91 PASS. (C4) Mean tier strictly increases: background=0.800 < aftershock=2.000 PASS. (C5) Aftershock tier set = {T2} only; T0=0 T1=0; background spans all three tiers (T0=56/T1=56/T2=28) PASS. (C6) Omori-Utsu decay certified: 7 consecutive daily aftershock event sums strictly decrease: 533>474>348>234>141>125>110 PASS. ORBIT MAPPING: background (21 events in 35 days = 0.15 events/6h) distributes across T0/T1/T2 (near-random); aftershock (1965 events in 7 days = 70.2 events/6h) 100% in T2. Ratio = 468x. Cosmos orbit = maximal seismic activity state (aftershock clustering). Theorem NT: earthquake locations/magnitudes are observer projections; event count per window is QA integer state; rank bins are integer arithmetic. 6 checks PASS; 8/8 fixtures PASS. Live read via USGS FDSNWS geojson API; hardcoded fallback window count arrays. First cert in chain using Poisson count (temporal density) as feature type. PRIMARY SOURCES: (Utsu, 1961) A statistical study of the occurrence of aftershocks, Geophys. Mag. 30, 521-605 (Omori-Utsu law); (Incorporated Research Institutions for Seismology, 2011) USGS ComCat doi:10.5066/F7MS3QZH; Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Witt tower theory). Structural parent: cert [110]. Empirical chain extends certs [442]-[447]. Validated 2026-06-18.
